channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Romance books recommended by NPR staff for summer reading
item.description: Summer is great for some sizzling reads and NPR staff have recommendations for romance novels from our Books We Love list.
item.content:encoded: Summer is great for some sizzling reads and NPR staff have recommendations for romance novels from our Books We Love list.
item.dc:creator: Wailin Wong

item.title: America's 250th carries somber note for forcibly displaced Indigenous groups
item.description: Every couple of years members of the Eastern Shawnee Tribe of Oklahoma travel back to visit ancestral and historic sites located in the Midwest. Members say it's especially poignant as the country celebrates the 250th year of American independence.
item.content:encoded: Every couple of years members of the Eastern Shawnee Tribe of Oklahoma travel back to visit ancestral and historic sites located in the Midwest. Members say it's especially poignant as the country celebrates the 250th year of American independence.
item.dc:creator: Adriana Martinez-Smiley

item.title: Japan seeks to form new centralized spy agency
item.description: NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Richard Samuels, professor of Political Science at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology, about Japan's effort to form a new centralized intelligence agency.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Richard Samuels, professor of Political Science at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology, about Japan's effort to form a new centralized intelligence agency.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Ofman

item.title: Making a great Hollywood epic is tough. A look at some of the genre's best
item.description: The team at Cineplexity debate the current state of Hollywood epics with the release of Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey.'
item.content:encoded: The team at Cineplexity debate the current state of Hollywood epics with the release of Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey.'
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: How researchers found a new species of monkey deep in the rainforest
item.description: In the Democratic Republic of Congo, a team of researchers discovered a new species of monkey and formed deep friendship along the way.
item.content:encoded: In the Democratic Republic of Congo, a team of researchers discovered a new species of monkey and formed deep friendship along the way.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: Humanitarian crisis in Lebanon deepens as hopes for lasting ceasefire fade
item.description: NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Abby Maxman, President of Oxfam America, about her recent visit to meet internally displaced people in Lebanon amid ongoing war in the region.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Abby Maxman, President of Oxfam America, about her recent visit to meet internally displaced people in Lebanon amid ongoing war in the region.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: An Israeli paraglider tries to save migrating swifts that nest in the Western Wall
item.description: Swifts have nested in Jerusalem's Western Wall since biblical times. Now one Israeli paraglider is trying to save them.
item.content:encoded:
Swifts have nested in Jerusalem's Western Wall since biblical times. Now one Israeli paraglider is trying to save them.

(Image credit: Ruth Sherlock)
item.dc:creator: Ruth Sherlock

item.title: How to make the complex world of skincare easy
item.description: NPR's Life Kit breaks down what you really need to know about skincare.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Life Kit breaks down what you really need to know about skincare.

item.title: What we can learn from the unique smell of wildfire smoke
item.description: NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Derek Mallia, professor University of Utah's Department of Atmospheric Sciences, about why wildfire smoke in the northeast has a distinctive smell.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Derek Mallia, professor University of Utah's Department of Atmospheric Sciences, about why wildfire smoke in the northeast has a distinctive smell.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: Former U.S. soccer phenom Jozy Altidore finds broadcast stardom on Telemundo
item.description: NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Telemundo's Jozy Altidore about the Spanish language broadcaster's ability to reach wide audiences with their World Cup coverage, even across language barriers.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Telemundo's Jozy Altidore about the Spanish language broadcaster's ability to reach wide audiences with their World Cup coverage, even across language barriers.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma
